step_0.prompt: Let's sort this out! 🔍 First: have you already completed the payment at the central panel (coins, card, or loyalty card)?

pay_help.prompt:
No problem! The payment sequence:
1️⃣ Pay at the central panel (coins / card / loyalty card)
2️⃣ Select your machine number
3️⃣ Press the program button
4️⃣ Close the door

The display will guide you at each step.

pay_retry.prompt: Were you able to complete the payment now? (yes / no)

transitionDescriptions.2: A price or amount in euros (e.g. 04.00, 12.00, 3.50) — shows how much is left to pay
transitionDescriptions.3: PUSH or Pr-3 — program selected but not confirmed
transitionDescriptions.4: DOOR — the display shows a door-related message
transitionDescriptions.5: ALM, alarm, ALN or any alarm code (not AL001)
transitionDescriptions.6: AL001, 001, error 001 — numeric sequence error code
transitionDescriptions.7: END — the wash cycle has finished normally

display_check.onInterruptFallback: Please describe what you see on the display 👀

case_sel.prompt:
SEL means the machine is ready and waiting 👍

👉 Select your machine NUMBER on the panel, then press any PROGRAM button.

case_price.prompt:
The display shows the remaining amount to pay 💶

First check: do you have remaining credit on the central panel?
👉 If yes — re-select your machine number and press the program button.
👉 If no — insert more coins or tap your card until the display shows PUSH.

case_push.prompt:
Payment complete! ✅ The display shows PUSH/Pr-3.

👉 Press any PROGRAM button to start the wash.

case_door.prompt:
The display shows 'door' 🚪 The door is not fully closed.

👉 Open it and close it firmly — the machine will start automatically.

case_alm.prompt: There's an alarm ⚠️ Which alarm code do you see on the display?

transitionDescriptions.1: ALM/A or ALM A — water inlet alarm
transitionDescriptions.2: ALM/E or ALM E — drainage alarm
transitionDescriptions.3: ALM/door or ALM DOOR — door alarm
transitionDescriptions.4: ALM/VAr or ALM VAR — variator/electrical alarm

case_alm.onInterruptFallback: Please describe the alarm code you see on the display ⚠️

alm_water.prompt: ALM/A = water inlet alarm 💧 Press STOP once to reset, then check the water supply is open and try again.

alm_drain.prompt: ALM/E = drainage alarm 🚰 Press STOP once to reset. Check there are no obstructions in the drain, then try again.

alm_door_fix.prompt: ALM/door = door alarm 🚪 Open and firmly close the door, then press STOP to reset the alarm.

alm_var.prompt: ALM/VAr = electrical variator alarm ⚡ Press STOP once to reset and try again.

alm_reset.prompt: Press STOP once to reset the alarm, then restart the full sequence from step 1 (payment).

alm_check.prompt: Has the alarm cleared and the machine started? (yes / no)

case_al001.prompt:
AL001 = sequence error ❗ You pressed the program button before paying. Restart the full sequence:

1️⃣ Pay → 2️⃣ Select machine number → 3️⃣ Press program → 4️⃣ Close door

case_end.prompt: END means the wash cycle has finished normally ✅ If there is a problem with your laundry, please describe it.

check_result.prompt: Has the machine started now? (yes / no)

check_retry.prompt: Let's try again. What does the display show now? 👀

transitionDescriptions.2: A price or amount in euros (e.g. 04.00, 12.00, 3.50) — shows how much is left to pay
transitionDescriptions.3: PUSH or Pr-3 — program selected but not confirmed
transitionDescriptions.4: DOOR — the display shows a door-related message
transitionDescriptions.5: ALM, alarm, ALN or any alarm code (not AL001)
transitionDescriptions.6: AL001, 001, error 001 — numeric sequence error code
transitionDescriptions.7: END — the wash cycle has finished normally

check_retry.onInterruptFallback: Please describe what you see on the display 👀

escalate_pay.prompt: I'm notifying the operator to help with the payment issue 🙌

escalate_alm.prompt: The alarm persists ⚠️ I'm notifying the operator — please try a different machine if possible.

escalate.prompt: I'm notifying the operator 🙌 They will assist you shortly.

ok.prompt: The washing machine has started! ✅ Your normal wash cycle takes approximately 28 minutes. Enjoy! 😊

step_0.prompt: I'm sorry to hear there's a problem after the wash 😔 What happened?

transitionDescriptions.1: Clothes still very wet, spin failed, laundry dripping
transitionDescriptions.2: Door still locked, can't open, door won't open
transitionDescriptions.3: Clothes appear damaged, ruined, torn, discolored
transitionDescriptions.4: Very little foam, no foam, worried about detergent
transitionDescriptions.5: Machine won't drain, water still inside, other issue

step_0.onInterruptFallback: Please describe what happened after the wash 😔

wet_clothes.prompt: Does the display show END+bAL? (yes / no)

bal_case.prompt:
END+bAL = load imbalance — the spin cycle could not complete ⚖️

The laundry needs to be separated and re-washed in 2 machines. I'm notifying the operator to assess the situation and any possible compensation.

wet_normal.prompt: The load may have been too large or unbalanced. I'm notifying the operator — they will assess whether a re-wash is needed.

door_locked.prompt: After the cycle, the door may take 2–3 minutes to unlock (longer if the machine is still draining) ⏱️ Please wait and try again.

door_locked_check.prompt: Has the door opened now? (yes / no)

damaged.prompt: I'm sorry about your clothes 😔 I'm notifying the operator — damaged clothes require individual assessment. No automatic compensation is guaranteed.

foam_info.prompt:
Little foam is completely normal! ✅

The machine uses industrial detergent with automatic dosing (detergent + softener + active oxygen). Industrial products generate less visible foam — this does NOT affect wash quality.

escalate_door.prompt: The door is still locked 🔒 I'm notifying the operator to assist you.

escalate.prompt: I'm notifying the operator 🙌 They will assist you shortly.

ok_post.prompt: Great! ✅ Glad it's resolved. If you need anything else, I'm here 😊

step_0.prompt:
I understand you pressed STOP ⛔

Was this the *first time* starting this wash cycle (you hadn't washed anything yet)?

stop_first_time.prompt: If STOP was pressed before the wash started, a free reactivation may be possible — but only the operator can authorize it. I'm notifying them now 🙌

stop_mid_cycle.prompt: If STOP was pressed during an active wash cycle, the cycle is cancelled. I'm notifying the operator to assess your case — no automatic compensation is guaranteed.
